subject: Gaming
title: The Paradox of Choice in Open Worlds
description: A fast breakdown of The Paradox of Choice in Open Worlds for builders.

hook.text: Too many options can paralyze you. Open-world games with 200 quests often feel less fun than linear ones.

buildup.text: Barry Schwartz's Paradox of Choice says more options create more anxiety and less satisfaction.

discovery.text: Ubisoft's map icons became a meme. Hundreds of markers on a map feel like a checklist, not an adventure.

twist.text: Breath of the Wild solved this by hiding content. No map markers—just curiosity and organic discovery.

climax.text: The best open worlds guide you without overwhelming you. Less information creates more wonder.

punchline.text: Two hundred markers feel like homework. No markers feel like adventure.

quiz.question: How did Breath of the Wild avoid the paradox of choice?
options: By hiding content and removing map markers to encourage organic discovery
